title: AI Meeting & Lecture Note Taker
tagline: Transforms long sessions into structured, searchable intelligence.
description: An AI-assisted note-taking system that captures meetings or lectures, transcribes speech, extracts decisions, and organizes summaries for review.
problem: Students and teams lose important details in long spoken sessions, especially when action items, definitions, and decisions are scattered across the conversation.
solution: The system converts audio into text, segments the transcript, and produces concise summaries, key points, and follow-up items through an AI processing layer.
highlights: Search-ready note structure for later retrieval
challenges: Handling noisy audio and long-form transcripts
Keeping summaries concise without losing important context
Designing a review flow that feels useful instead of overwhelming
outcomes: Reduced manual note cleanup after meetings and lectures
Created a foundation for future retrieval and study workflows
Demonstrated practical AI product thinking across frontend, backend, and model orchestration

title: Wildlife Conservation & Species Identification
tagline: Computer vision support for field conservation workflows.
description: A research-oriented species identification system designed to help classify wildlife imagery and support conservation monitoring.
problem: Conservation teams often work with large volumes of camera-trap or field images that are slow to classify manually.
solution: The project applies computer vision classification to identify likely species, record metadata, and prepare reviewed observations for conservation analysis.
highlights: Model-ready image workflow for field observations
Designed for human review before conservation use
challenges: Managing varied lighting, motion blur, and partial animal visibility
Representing model confidence clearly for non-technical users
Balancing research flexibility with product-level interface clarity
outcomes: Established an AI research pattern for image classification projects
Created a recruiter-friendly example of mission-driven computer vision
Prepared the system for dataset expansion and future analytics

title: AI Plant Disease Detection
tagline: Early crop-health screening through image intelligence.
description: An image-based AI workflow that helps identify plant disease patterns from leaf photos and returns practical next-step guidance.
problem: Farmers and gardeners may miss early disease signals or lack immediate access to expert diagnosis.
solution: The application accepts plant images, applies disease classification, and presents likely conditions with confidence, prevention notes, and care recommendations.
highlights: Practical care guidance paired with model output
challenges: Keeping the workflow fast on common device images
outcomes: Demonstrates responsible AI communication in a high-impact domain
Adds another strong computer vision case study to the project layer
Prepared for future dataset growth and model evaluation tracking

title: CrowdScope Crowd Monitoring System
tagline: Operational crowd intelligence for density and safety awareness.
description: A crowd monitoring concept that combines data ingestion, vision-assisted counting, and dashboard metrics for public-space awareness.
problem: Event and facility operators need timely visibility into crowd density, hotspots, and trend changes before conditions become unsafe.
solution: CrowdScope structures camera or sensor inputs into monitored zones, computes density signals, and surfaces active system metrics for operators.
highlights: Backend-first architecture prepared for streaming inputs
Dashboard-ready metrics for active system visibility
challenges: Designing a scalable event ingestion model
Turning raw detections into useful operational signals
outcomes: Shows system design judgment beyond a single model demo
Connects AI, backend, and dashboard engineering in one workflow
Prepared for future simulation data and GitHub analytics integration
